root_text.sn47.5:1.3: “‘akusalarāsī’ti, bhikkhave, vadamāno pañca nīvaraṇe sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn47.5:1.4: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, akusalarāsi, yadidaṁ—pañca nīvaraṇā.
root_text.sn47.5:1.7: ‘Akusalarāsī’ti, bhikkhave, vadamāno ime pañca nīvaraṇe sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn47.5:1.8: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, akusalarāsi, yadidaṁ—pañca nīvaraṇā.
root_text.sn47.5:2.1: ‘Kusalarāsī’ti, bhikkhave, vadamāno cattāro satipaṭṭhāne sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn47.5:2.2: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kusalarāsi, yadidaṁ—cattāro satipaṭṭhānā.
root_text.sn47.5:2.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.5:2.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.5:2.8: ‘Kusalarāsī’ti, bhikkhave, vadamāno ime cattāro satipaṭṭhāne sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn47.5:2.9: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, kusalarāsi, yadidaṁ—cattāro satipaṭṭhānā”ti.

translation_text.sn47.5:1.3: “Rightly speaking, mendicants, you’d call these five hindrances a ‘heap of the unskillful’.
translation_text.sn47.5:1.4: For these five hindrances are entirely a heap of the unskillful.
translation_text.sn47.5:1.6: The hindrances of sensual desire, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.sn47.5:1.7: Rightly speaking, you’d call these five hindrances a ‘heap of the unskillful’.
translation_text.sn47.5:1.8: For these five hindrances are entirely a heap of the unskillful.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.1: Rightly speaking, you’d call these four kinds of mindfulness meditation a ‘heap of the skillful’.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.2: For these four kinds of mindfulness meditation are entirely a heap of the skillful.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.4: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.5: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.5:2.6: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind …
translation_text.sn47.5:2.7: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.8: Rightly speaking, you’d call these four kinds of mindfulness meditation a ‘heap of the skillful’.
translation_text.sn47.5:2.9: For these four kinds of mindfulness meditation are entirely a heap of the skillful.”

reference_text.sn47.5:1.1: csp1ed14.127, csp2ed14.127, ms14S5_768, msdiv371, ndp15.126, pts-vp-pli5.146
